intro_text:
Maybe you've noticed you're drinking more than you used to. Or working longer hours to avoid feeling what's underneath. Or your relationship is struggling because old patterns keep showing up in new ways. You know something has to change. You're here because you're finally ready to figure out what that something is.

I'm Kaylie, and I specialize in helping adults and couples in Leesburg, Virginia unpack the trauma that's been running the show. Together, we'll work through the anxiety, depression, and attachment wounds that keep you stuck in cycles you desperately want to break.
intro_title: You're exhausted from managing everyone else's expectations while your own needs sit on the back burner.

resources_items.title: Constantly feeling like you're waiting for the other shoe to drop.
resources_items.content: Even when life is fairly predictable or even calm, your nervous system is scanning for a crisis. It’s that subtle humming of anxiety in your chest or the inability to truly relax after a day of work. You’ve become an expert at pre-fixing problems that haven't even happened yet.

resources_items.title: Struggling to separate your worth from your productivity.
resources_items.content: It feels like you have to be high-performing just to be worthy of being in the room. You might be the person everyone leans on, but inside, you’re terrified that if you slow down or make a mistake, the whole facade will crumble and people will see you the way *you* see you.

resources_items.title: Being an expert at reading the room ... at the expense of your own needs.
resources_items.content: You're incredibly attuned to other people’s moods, shifting the way you show up to keep the peace or stay safe. You often leave interactions feeling exhausted, wondering if you actually liked the person or if you were just busy making sure *they* liked *you*.

resources_items.title: Feeling like a "floating head," disconnected from your physical self.
resources_items.content: When things get intense, you might feel numb or checked out, as if you’re watching your life through a thick pane of glass. It’s hard to know what you’re feeling in your body until you’re already at a breaking point or completely burnt out.

resources_items.title: Carrying a quiet, heavy sense that you are fundamentally difficult or broken.
resources_items.content: Beyond just low self-esteem, it’s a deep-seated belief that your needs are a burden to others. You apologize for things that aren't your fault and hesitate to ask for help because you don't want to take up too much space in the world.

resources_items.title: Using "doing" to avoid "being" — keeping your world busy so you don't have to feel.
resources_items.content: Whether it’s staying up late to work, the endless scroll on your phone, or that extra (extra) glass of wine to take the edge off, you have a toolkit of habits designed to keep your feelings at bay. It’s a constant race to stay one step ahead of a restlessness you can't quite name. You're exhausted from trying to outrun your own internal landscape.

resources_text: Developmental trauma doesn't always look like what you see in movies. Sometimes it looks like:
callout_title: You don't have to struggle alone.
trauma_therapist_text: As a trauma therapist in Leesburg, I work with adults who are ready to understand why they do what they do and finally create lasting change.
adaptations_text: These aren't character flaws. They're adaptations you developed to survive situations that were too much to handle at the time. The problem? What helped you survive back then is now keeping you from truly living.
wounds_header: When Old Wounds Keep Showing Up in Your Present

how_to_start_items.title: Consultation
how_to_start_items.text: Schedule a free 15-minute phone consultation. We'll talk about what you're dealing with and whether we're a good fit.

how_to_start_items.title: Intake
how_to_start_items.text: Book your first session if it feels right. No pressure, no sales pitch.

how_to_start_items.title: Sessions
how_to_start_items.text: Begin the work of understanding yourself, healing old wounds, and building a life that aligns with your values and desires.

kaylie_bio:
My approach is warm, direct, and grounded in evidence-based practices. I won't just nod sympathetically while you talk. I'll challenge you when you need it, celebrate your progress, and help you connect the dots between your past and present.

I believe healing happens *in relationship*. That means our therapeutic relationship matters just as much as any techniques we use.

Outside of sessions, I'm committed to ongoing training and staying current with trauma research. Your healing deserves a therapist who never stops learning.
feature_title: Reclaiming Your Narrative in a Space Built for Healing.
kaylie_subtitle: Owner, Board-Certified Licensed Professional Counselor, EMDR Certified Therapist
hero_title: You've Been Holding It Together for Everyone Else. What About *You?*

is_this_you_items.title: Individual Adults
is_this_you_items.body: You're successful on paper but feel empty inside. You've spent decades taking care of others and lost yourself along the way. You're ready to figure out who you are outside of your roles and responsibilities.

is_this_you_items.title: Couples Therapy
is_this_you_items.subtitle: (Including Non-Monogamous and Polyamorous Relationships)
is_this_you_items.body: Your relationship is struggling under the weight of unspoken resentments, different attachment styles, or past trauma showing up in your partnership. Whether you're monogamous, non-monogamous, or polyamorous, I provide judgment-free couples therapy that honors your relationship structure.

is_this_you_items.title: Unique Relationship Dynamics
is_this_you_items.body:
Many therapists aren't trained in the unique dynamics of consensual non-monogamy. I am. If you're navigating jealousy, boundary challenges, or communication breakdowns in your non-traditional relationship, you don't have to explain yourself here.

I offer both in-person therapy in Leesburg, VA and virtual therapy throughout Virginia.

feature_text: You’ve spent years mastering the art of "fine," but underneath, the weight of it all is starting to feel heavy. At Wildwood, we move beyond traditional talk therapy. By integrating EMDR and IFS-informed parts work, we help you process the roots of your exhaustion so you can finally stop holding it together and start *living*. This is your space to be human, not just productive.
final_cta_tagline: TAKE A BREATH. YOU’RE IN THE RIGHT PLACE.
root_title: Therapy That Actually Gets to the Root
root_content:
You've probably tried therapy before. Maybe it felt like venting without real progress. Or your therapist was nice but didn't push you enough.

I blend EMDR therapy, Internal Family Systems (IFS), mindfulness, DBT, and ACT to create a treatment approach that's tailored to you.

EMDR (Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing) helps your brain reprocess traumatic memories so they stop hijacking your present. I'm EMDR Certified, which means I've completed advanced training beyond basic certification.

Parts Work (IFS-informed) helps you understand the different parts of yourself that are often in conflict. The part that wants to be vulnerable and the part that's terrified of getting hurt. The part that's angry and the part that feels guilty for being angry.

Mindfulness, DBT, and ACT give you practical tools to regulate your nervous system, manage intense emotions, and build a life that aligns with your values.

**This isn't about fixing you. You're not broken. It's about healing the wounds that convinced you that you were.**
callout_text:
Life can sometimes feel unbearable. Maybe you’ve tried dealing with your issues by talking to close friends, searching internet rabbit holes, compartmentalizing, and/or distracting yourself with things like scrolling, substances, or spending.

Wherever you go, though, there you are. The issue remains, and though you may have had temporary relief, you feel more alone, even shameful, afterward.

Whether you’re experiencing new challenges or iterations of long-standing ones, therapy can help. You deserve to feel seen, heard, and supported. You can have a more balanced, joyful existence.

**Healing is possible, and I can help.**
hero_subtitle: EMDR and Trauma-Informed Therapy for adults and couples in Leesburg, VA
